# LLM Daily Review

> Automated daily discovery and evaluation of LLM apps posted on Hacker News.
> Every day at 15:00 UTC, we test real LLM applications in isolated Docker containers
> and score them across 11 criteria. Free, open source, no login required.

## What we do

- Scrape Hacker News daily for new LLM-related app submissions
- Test each app in an isolated Docker container (sandboxed, safe)
- Score on 11 criteria: novelty, functionality, UX, differentiation, performance, docs, security, monetization potential, community fit, maintenance signals, and technical depth
- Publish scores with recommendation badges: Strong candidate, Worth watching, Niche, Skip
- Send weekly Top 5 digest via newsletter

## Why it matters

Hacker News posts 50-200 new items daily. Finding the genuinely useful LLM tools requires hours of manual testing. We automate that.

## Data & Access

- Web UI: https://tokenstree.eu/
- Sitemap: https://tokenstree.eu/sitemap.xml
- Newsletter: https://tokenstree.eu/newsletter/
- GitHub (open source): https://github.com/vfalbor/llm-daily-review
- API (results by date): https://tokenstree.eu/api/results?date=YYYY-MM-DD
- API (calendar): https://tokenstree.eu/api/calendar?year=YYYY&month=MM

## Scoring criteria (11 dimensions, weighted, normalized to 100)

1. **Novelty** (0-10): How different is this from existing tools?
2. **Functionality**: Does it actually work end-to-end?
3. **UX/DX**: Is the interface or API well-designed?
4. **Differentiation**: Clear value proposition?
5. **Performance**: Speed and reliability
6. **Documentation**: README, examples, guides
7. **Security**: Auth, data handling, privacy
8. **Monetization potential**: Business model clarity
9. **Community fit**: Relevant to HN audience
10. **Maintenance signals**: Active commits, issue responses
11. **Technical depth**: Code quality, architecture decisions

## Recommendation thresholds

- ⭐ Strong candidate: Score ≥78 AND novelty ≥7
- 👀 Worth watching: Score ≥57
- 🔍 Niche: Score 35–56
- ⏭ Skip: Score <35 or differentiation ≤3

## Stack

Node.js 20, Express, SQLite (better-sqlite3), Docker (for sandboxed app testing), Claude API (scoring), Nodemailer (newsletter)

## License

CC BY 4.0 — fork and adapt the scoring skills freely.
